description: Lock Tally Marks to source-backed Paleolithic incised/notched artifacts rather than broad record keeping, proto-writing, or proven arithmetic.

checks.reason: The launch-quality date is anchored to the Border Cave early Later Stone Age notched/incised bone horizon rather than the old Ancient era-default placeholder.
The dated source-backed anchor is a worked bone artifact, but tally-like devices can also be made on other durable media.
Tally-like quantity marks follow earlier durable graphic symbolism without being a hard dependency of every counted mark.
Paleolithic tally-like notches must not be reinterpreted as later Near Eastern proto-writing.
The node covers pre-writing tally-like marks and should remain upstream of formal writing systems.
Mesopotamian-style economic record keeping is a downstream institutional use, not a prerequisite for Paleolithic tally-like artifacts.
